TextENGNew: After dinner, I didn't even have the energy to watch TV... I crawled under the futon in my room... and let gloomy thoughts consume me...
I had told Rika-chan absolutely everything.
...In the moment, she had seemed so reassuring... but had that really been the right decision...?
changeReason: Old translates 頼もしい as 'trustworthy' (a character judgment); new uses 'reassuring' (an emotional response), which changes the narrator's implied reasoning.

TextENGNew: The more I replayed it in my mind... the more baffling Rika-chan's words became.
......She didn't merely know about Takano-san and the others' deaths... she spoke as though she were personally involved.
changeReason: Old specifies 'Takano-san and Tomitake-san' by name; new uses vaguer 'Takano-san and the others,' changing who is explicitly referenced.

TextENGNew: ...Even now, so long after the fact... lying under my futon... a violent trembling seized me...
Had I been... drawn in by Rika-chan's sweet smile... and said things I never should have...?
...I should have just kept playing dumb...
I never should have let her see my weakness...
A tangle of regret and fear... made the darkness of night all the more terrifying.
A sudden knock!!
......I might have screamed.@k That's how badly it startled me.
``Keiichi!@k@vS02/00/132600002. I've been calling you!@k@vS02/00/132600003. You have a phone call.@k@vS02/00/132600004. ...From Sonozaki-san.``
It was Dad.
He held the cordless phone out to me through the crack in the door.
changeReason: Old has narrator reaching for phone; new has father holding it out -- different subject.

TextENGNew: ``S-Sonozaki-san... which one?@k@vS02/01/130100512. The older sister?@k@vS02/01/130100513. Or the younger...?``
``...How should I know?@k@vS02/00/132600006. Pick it up and find out yourself.``
...I took the receiver... and burrowed back under my futon.
I bolted upright.
...It was Shion--the person I had been so desperately hoping would call again last night.
``Shion! ...Um, ...about last night... I'm sorry.@k@vS02/01/130100518. ...I lost my temper...``
I heard what sounded like a deep sigh from the other end of the line...

``...We're in the same boat, you and I.@k@vS02/01/130100520. ...I shouldn't have put all the blame on you like that.@k@vS02/01/130100521. ...I won't do it again.@k@vS02/01/130100522. I'm apologizing, so please forgive me...``
``I called because I'm over it.@k@vS02/06/130600290. ...If you're truly sorry, then I forgive you--so please stop apologizing.``
Shion's tone still carried a hint of irritation, but at least she had said she would forgive me.
``...As you said, Kei-chan, we share the same fate.@k@vS02/06/130600292. Confirming each other's safety is the only guarantee we have.``
``...Yeah... I couldn't agree more.``
``...That's why we need to share whatever information we have.@k@vS02/06/130600294. ...So we don't end up like Takano-san and Tomitake-san.``
...Gulp.@k ...I forced down a hard lump of saliva.
``Then... let me pick up where we left off yesterday.@k@vS02/06/130600296. ...You'll hear me out without getting angry this time, right?``
``First of all.@k@vS02/06/130600298. ......It seems that sneaking into the ritual storehouse was an even greater taboo than we realized.``
............The gruesome ends that Takano-san and the others met--terrible enough to call them a warning to others--were more than enough to make me believe that...
changeReason: Old specifies 'Takano-san and Tomitake-san' by name; new uses 'Takano-san and the others,' altering who is explicitly referenced.

TextENGNew: ``......The culprits killed those two, the ringleaders who defiled the storehouse... and we're next.@k@vS02/06/130600300. ...I know you don't want to believe it... but you have to accept it.@k@vS02/06/130600301. ...By the time you've disappeared, it'll be too late to start believing me, you know?!``
``I-I know.@k@vS02/01/130100527. ...You can never be too careful, after all.``
``I don't... want to accept it either.@k@vS02/06/130600303. ...The idea that just peeking at a collection of torture instruments warrants being killed like that... I refuse to believe it...!``
......But that's what a taboo is.
...To those who hold it sacred, breaking the taboo is unforgivable... no matter how trivial or innocent our intentions may have been.

``...Let's agree to report anything that strikes us as suspicious.@k@vS02/06/130600305. ...If we piece together all the small fragments we each have... we might figure out who killed them... no, not just this year's incident.``
``We might find the key to solving all the past incidents as well.``
changeReason: Old says 'resolve everything'; new specifies 'the past incidents as well,' adding explicit reference to solving previous cases, which matches the JPN 過去の連続事件.

TextENGNew: ``...Mm... you're right.@k@vS02/01/130100529. ...You're absolutely right.``
Here I was, doing nothing but cowering in fear... while Shion had already been thinking so proactively...
...I felt both reassured by her resolve and ashamed of my own passiveness...
changeReason: Old translates 頼もしい as 'trustworthy'; new renders it as 'reassured by her resolve,' shifting from a judgment of character to an emotional response.

TextENGNew: ``...Then, since I'm the one who suggested it, I'll go first.``
``...Lately, I've had the feeling that someone is watching me.``
``......It might just be my imagination, though.@k@vS02/06/130600310. ...But I thought I should report it anyway.``

``...Right now I think it's just me being paranoid... but if you've been feeling the same way, Kei-chan, like someone is watching you...@k@vS02/06/130600312. ...then maybe it's not just in my head...``
``W-Well... don't worry about that.@k@vS02/01/130100532. ...At least on my end... everything's been fine.``
...I think.
......As I talked with Shion, I mentally retraced my entire day.
...I had absolutely no basis to claim that everything was fine...
``......I see.@k@vS02/06/130600314. ...Then let's call it my imagination... but please stay on your guard, Kei-chan.@k@vS02/06/130600315. Be especially careful when you're walking around alone.

...I live in Okinomiya, so I'm rarely by myself, but you're in Hinamizawa.@k@vS02/06/130600317. ...There must be plenty of times when you're on your own.@k@vS02/06/130600318. ...Please, be extra cautious.``
``......You're right.@k@vS02/01/130100534. Yeah... I'll be careful.``
``...Also... about my sister... Have you noticed anything different about her?``
``Your sister... you mean Mion.@k@vS02/01/130100536. ...What do you mean by 'different'...?``
``...The other day, she cornered me and demanded to know where I was on the night of Watanagashi.``
changeReason: Old says '先日' as 'yesterday'; new translates as 'the other day' (correct per JPN). Old says 'asked'; new says 'cornered me and demanded,' which better reflects 問い質す (to interrogate).

TextENGNew: ``What?! She... she asked me the same thing.@k@vS02/01/130100538. ...The day before yesterday, I think... Mion asked me...``
``......I thought so.@k@vS02/06/130600322. ......I told her I wasn't there.@k@vS02/06/130600323. ...What about you?``
changeReason: Old translates やっぱりですか as 'I see' (neutral acknowledgment); new uses 'I thought so' (implies prior expectation), which is more accurate to the JPN.

TextENGNew: ``Same here.@k@vS02/01/130100540. ...I deflected.``
...We both exchanged a quiet sigh of relief.
``...Ever since the night of the festival, my sister's behavior has been off.@k@vS02/06/130600325. ...Better safe than sorry... so please keep your guard up, okay?``
......Mion's behavior, huh...
changeReason: Old states 'Mion was acting strangely' (assertion); new is 'Mion's behavior, huh' (reflective musing), which better matches the JPN 魅音の様子、か (trailing particle か indicating rumination).

TextENGNew: .........Apart from her interrogating me about the night of Watanagashi...... she hadn't seemed all that different to me.@k ...At least, not as far as I could tell.
changeReason: Old says 'asking me'; new says 'interrogating me,' conveying a more forceful tone that better matches the JPN 詰問 (cross-examine/interrogate).

TextENGNew: ...But her twin sister was saying she'd been acting strangely.
...Perhaps she could sense subtle changes that an outsider like me would never notice...
``......Got it.@k@vS02/01/130100542. ...I'll keep my guard up.@k@vS02/01/130100543. ...Rena walks home with me every day, so I shouldn't end up alone with Mion too often...``
``Please be careful.@k@vS02/06/130600327. ...And if anything worries you, let me know.``
``Understood.``
``...So then, is there anything on your end that you want to tell me, Kei-chan?``
......My turn, huh.
......That's right--I should probably mention that Ooishi-san asked me the same kinds of questions at the library.
``...Oh, that time when you couldn't get away fast enough?``

``Yeah.@k@vS02/01/130100546. Ooishi-san pressed me pretty hard.@k@vS02/01/130100547. ...He wanted to know whether I'd been with Takano-san and the others that night, whether I'd met with you.@k@vS02/01/130100548. ...It seemed like Ooishi-san had seen the four of us together.``
changeReason: Old specifies 'Takano-san and Tomitake-san'; new uses 'Takano-san and the others,' altering the explicit reference. The JPN 鷹野さんたち uses -tachi which is ambiguous but typically includes Tomitake.

TextENGNew: ``...From the police's perspective, that would make us the last people to have seen them alive.@k@vS02/06/130600331. It's only natural they'd take an interest in us, I suppose.``
changeReason: New adds 'alive' which is not in the old translation, clarifying the implication that they were last seen by these characters before their deaths.

TextENGNew: ``...I dodged his questions too... but that probably only made him more suspicious.``
changeReason: Old says 'made them more interested'; new says 'made him more suspicious' -- 'interested' and 'suspicious' carry different connotations about Ooishi's stance.

TextENGNew: There was no way I could have fooled that wily old fox Ooishi-san.
......My flustered reaction must have only strengthened his convictions...

``Hey, Shion.@k@vS02/01/130100551. ...Since it's the police... shouldn't we just come clean...?@k@vS02/01/130100552. I mean, given our situation, I'd think having the police on our side would only be a good thing...``
Shion didn't respond right away.
...For a while, all I could hear was her long, deliberating sigh.

``...Come to think of it, Ooishi-san mentioned that he has some kind of suspicion about Mion... at least, I'm pretty sure that's what he said.@k@vS02/01/130100554. ......Yeah.@k@vS02/01/130100555. ...He definitely said that.``
``...As I told you yesterday, the Sonozaki family wields enormous influence over Hinamizawa.
......Ooishi is convinced that the string of mysterious deaths is the work of the entire village, with the Sonozaki family at its center.``
changeReason: Old says Ooishi 'is under the impression'; new says he 'is utterly convinced,' significantly changing the degree of Ooishi's certainty about the Sonozaki involvement.

TextENGNew: ``...The entire village... with the Sonozaki family at the center...?!``
...Please, don't fly off the handle like yesterday--just listen.
She cut me off before I could get worked up.
changeReason: Old has narrator as subject ('I hammered that home'); Japanese has Shion as subject -- completely different agent.

TextENGNew: ``...During the Hinamizawa Dam War, the shrine priest--whom everyone expected to lead the resistance--turned out to be a fence-sitter, so the Sonozaki main house took charge and rallied the village.@k@vS02/06/130600335. ...My sister told you about all those battles, didn't she?``
changeReason: Old says the priest 'acted as though he were unrelated'; new says he 'turned out to be a fence-sitter' (日和見). 'Unrelated' implies detachment while 'fence-sitter' implies indecisiveness, which better matches the JPN.

TextENGNew: ``...Yeah.@k@vS02/01/130100558. The whole village united and fought as one.@k@vS02/01/130100559. Protests, lawsuits, TV appearances...``

``In reality... far more extreme things were happening behind the scenes.@k@vS02/06/130600337. The Sonozaki main house is said to have carried out all manner of illegal resistance activities beneath the surface.``
``......Illegal resistance...?``
``For example, they would sneak into the construction site at night and steal equipment, sabotage machinery, or drop sugar cubes into the gasoline tanks of the heavy construction vehicles.``
``...Sugar in the gasoline tanks??@k@vS02/01/130100562. Why would they do that...``
``You don't know?!@k@vS02/06/130600340. It's a classic guerrilla tactic--the French Resistance used it during the war.@k@vS02/06/130600341. Sugar in the engine causes it to seize up and break.``
......That's... actually pretty serious, isn't it...?
``Once security at the construction site was tightened, they shifted to targeting key personnel involved in the project.``

``...For instance, the officials who authorized the construction received all manner of threats.@k@vS02/06/130600344. Not just prefectural bureaucrats, either--apparently it reached all the way up to high-ranking officials in the Ministry of Construction.``
``...From what I've heard... they even resorted to kidnapping a child.``
``K-Kidnapping?!?!``

``Yes.@k@vS02/06/130600347. The child of a senior Ministry of Construction official in charge of the Hinamizawa Dam was spirited away.@k@vS02/06/130600348. ...Then one day, out of nowhere, the child turned up safe in the Takatsudo mountains upstream from Hinamizawa.``

``...Officially, there was never any criminal statement claiming responsibility... but people whispered that it was a threat to halt the dam construction.@k@vS02/06/130600350. ...And there are countless other stories of threats and intimidation--too many to list.``
changeReason: Old says 'criminal accusations' but 犯行声明 means 'statement claiming criminal responsibility.'

TextENGNew: ``...That's... yeah, that's... pretty alarming...``

``It was right in the middle of all that...@k@vS02/06/130600352. ...that the first incident happened.@k@vS02/06/130600353. ...The dismemberment murder of the construction foreman.@k@vS02/06/130600354. ...People whispered that the Sonozaki family bribed the perpetrator... but of course, the man is still at large to this day.``
changeReason: Old says 'construction manager'; new says 'construction foreman' (現場監督 literally means site supervisor/foreman). Also adds 'murder' for clarity.

TextENGNew: ......When she put it like that...... the Sonozaki family really did look suspicious.

``Ooishi seems to believe that they 'eliminated' the culprit as well, to keep him quiet.@k@vS02/06/130600356. ...And who can blame him?@k@vS02/06/130600357. ...Even I, a member of the family...@k@vS02/06/130600358. ...suspect that the Sonozaki main house was pulling strings behind the scenes.``
``......Are you... telling me to believe all that...?@k@vS02/01/130100566. ...That Mion's family, the Sonozaki main house, has been doing such terrible things... you want me to believe that...?``
``Even if you don't believe it, Kei-chan, the villagers do.@k@vS02/06/130600360. ...While they waged their clean, lawful resistance, the Sonozaki house did the dirty work on their behalf.
...Everyone believes the Sonozakis stood on the front lines and bore the brunt of it all.@k@vS02/06/130600362. ...That's why they're revered as something like dark heroes.``

``What I know is probably just the tip of the iceberg.@k@vS02/06/130600364. ...The really dangerous secrets, like the kidnapping, are known only to a handful of people--including Grandma, the family head.@k@vS02/06/130600365. ...The Sonozaki family is nothing if not secretive.``
``......Secretive, huh...``
......A family so deep and opaque that even Shion, one of their own, couldn't see the bottom.@k The Sonozaki family.
...And unlike Shion, her twin sister Mion sat squarely at the center of it all... which was a deeply unsettling thought...
``Despite her youth, my sister was the one who spearheaded all of those illegal resistance activities.``
``Mion was...?!@k@vS02/01/130100570. Wh-What do you mean...?``
``Well, our father is a big-shot yakuza boss.@k@vS02/06/130600368. ...So even as a child, my sister could command a bunch of young toughs under her.``
changeReason: Old says 'subjugate a bunch of young street rats'; new says 'command a bunch of young toughs under her.' 'Subjugate' implies forceful domination vs 'command' implies authority; 'street rats' vs 'toughs' characterize the subordinates differently.

TextENGNew: ``She was picked up by the police multiple times... though you'd never guess it from how easygoing she is nowadays.@k@vS02/06/130600372. ...Since she was a minor, they always released her right away.``
``...She seemed to understand that her age was a weapon she could use.``
...I probably shouldn't have, but... I let out a dry chuckle.
...Even as a child... that was so very Mion.
Her craftiness had apparently been on display from a young age...
``This is no laughing matter!!@k@vS02/06/130600375. Please, be serious!!``
``Ah, ...sorry. ...I didn't mean to...``
Shion scolded me sharply.

``...Well, that delinquent girl grew up to become a key figure in the Sonozaki main house before anyone noticed.@k@vS02/06/130600377. ...You can't really blame Ooishi for suspecting them in the dismemberment case and every incident since.``
............Hearing these stories of Mion's past... I sat there in stunned silence for a while.
None of it matched the image of Mion I had in my head.
...A young Mion, using every means at her disposal to fight the dam project.
changeReason: Old adds 'dirtying her hands with crimes' not in the Japanese あらゆる手段を駆使して (using every means).

TextENGNew: ...And as heir to the Sonozaki main house, always sitting at the very center of the strange incidents that occurred year after year...
...Was that... really the Mion I knew...?
Was the real Mion... someone I didn't know at all...?
I couldn't tell.
...I simply couldn't accept this portrait of Mion being painted by someone else.
``Well, let me get back on track.@k@vS02/06/130600379. ...I just wanted you to understand that my sister is someone worth being wary of.@k@vS02/06/130600380. ...Please, be careful.``
.........I didn't want to understand.
......Mion was a wonderful friend.
...There's no way she would ever do anything to corner me like that...
...True, she did grill me about the night of the festival... but even that... it wasn't as though she'd said it in a particularly harsh way or anything......
``......All right.@k@vS02/01/130100574. ...I'll try to be careful.``

``My sister and I are blood-related... but within the Sonozaki family, our standing is worlds apart.@k@vS02/06/130600382. ...Please understand that my position is very different from hers.@k@vS02/06/130600383. ...I'm asking you.``
......The more Shion told me about Mion... the less I felt I truly knew her.
...The Mion I knew... wasn't someone dangerous like that...
...She was goofy, and fun... passionate and spirited............
Knowing full well that I saw Mion that way, Shion was telling me to reconsider.
changeReason: Old implies the narrator just realized his view; new says Shion already knew. JPN says 俺が...認識があるのを知った上で (knowing I held such a view), confirming Shion's awareness, not the narrator's realization.

TextENGNew: .........To be wary of my best friend.@k ...To doubt her.
...It was profoundly sad... and painful...

``Now, about Ooishi.@k@vS02/06/130600385. ...If you think it's wise, I believe we should provide him with the information he needs.@k@vS02/06/130600386. ...Having the police on our side should work to our advantage.``
``...I was thinking the same thing, Shion...!``
``...The thing is, though... as I just explained, the Sonozaki family is already on the police's radar.

Ooishi in particular is utterly convinced that they're the masterminds behind the serial incidents.@k@vS02/06/130600389. ...That includes me, of course.@k@vS02/06/130600390. ...So... I'd rather not get too involved with Ooishi.``
......Right.
...If Ooishi-san knew about the Sonozaki family's shadowy history... he probably wouldn't give Shion a very warm welcome either.
changeReason: Old translates 暗闘 as 'strife-filled'; new uses 'shadowy.' JPN 暗闘 means 'secret struggle/covert conflict,' so 'shadowy' better captures the covert nature.

TextENGNew: ...Now, at last, I understood why Mion despised Ooishi-san so much.
changeReason: Old says 'instinctive dislike' (innate/unexplained); new says 'despised' with understanding of 'why' (reasoned). JPN 毛嫌い means visceral/gut-level dislike, but the difference in framing ('instinctive' vs understood 'why') changes the narrator's insight.

TextENGNew: ``...He probably already knows, but try to keep my name out of it.@k@vS02/06/130600392. ...As long as you do that, I don't see any problem with you telling him whatever you like.``
``Got it.@k@vS02/01/130100577. ...I'll keep your name out of it.``
``...The police's investigative resources should be a very reliable weapon for us right now.@k@vS02/06/130600394. ...If Ooishi tells you anything interesting, please share it with me.``

``Yeah, of course.@k@vS02/01/130100579. We'll share information and investigate this together.@k@vS02/01/130100580. ......Who killed Takano-san and Tomitake-san?@k@vS02/01/130100581. ...And who is coming after us?@k@vS02/01/130100582. ...Until we know the answer... we have to protect ourselves...``
changeReason: Old uses past tense 'Who was after us?'; new uses present tense 'who is coming after us?' The present tense conveys ongoing threat versus past threat.

TextENGNew: ``...Is there anything else?@k@vS02/06/130600396. Besides what Ooishi told you, I mean.@k@vS02/06/130600397. ...Please tell me about anything that's happened to you, no matter how small.``
...Besides my encounter with Ooishi-san, huh.
...Oh... should I tell her... that I confided everything to Rika-chan?
...Rika-chan had been acting as though she knew something.
......She told me to leave it to her... but what exactly did she mean..........
That's right...
When I talked to Rika-chan... I had mentioned Shion's name too.
...Had that been a mistake...?
......I had a feeling Shion would be angry about that...
``...Nothing else?@k@vS02/06/130600399. ...Kei-chan?``
I had been silent for so long that Shion, unable to bear the wait, prompted me to continue.
While I stumbled over my words, Shion spoke up instead.
``If there's nothing, then let me ask you something.@k@vS02/06/130600401. ...I just heard this myself...... Is it true that old man Kimiyoshi has gone missing?``
...Old man Kimiyoshi?@k ...I instinctively knew that was the village mayor's name.
``You mean the village mayor...?@k@vS02/01/130100585. ...Wait, Shion... you haven't heard?``
``I don't know anything about it!@k@vS02/06/130600403. ...I just happened to overhear my dad talking about it on the phone.``
changeReason: Old includes 'again' (また盗み聞いた=eavesdropped again); new omits 'again' and says 'happened to overhear,' losing the implication this is a repeated behavior.

TextENGNew: I realized too late that I shouldn't have said that.
......In this village of Hinamizawa... events that occurred around the time of Oyashiro-sama's curse... tended to be hushed up.

``...Yeah.@k@vS02/01/130100587. Actually... last night, after a meeting, he never came home, and the whole village was in an uproar.@k@vS02/01/130100588. They searched all over, but I haven't heard that they've found him...@k@vS02/01/130100589. The police should be looking for him too, but......``
changeReason: Old says village was 'confused'; new says 'in an uproar.' JPN 大騒ぎ means commotion/uproar, so 'in an uproar' is more accurate.

TextENGNew: ``Wh-Why didn't you tell me something that important right away?!!!!``
She yelled at me, raw emotion laid bare...!
``S-Sorry... I thought you already knew...@k@vS02/01/130100591. I wasn't trying to hide it...!``
The receiver fell silent.
...Had Shion... gotten angry again and hung up...?!
``Hey, Shion! Hello?!``
Shion's thin, bewildered voice sounded like it belonged to an entirely different person from the one who had been shouting just moments ago.
``...What happened?@k@vS02/01/130100594. ...Tell me.@k@vS02/01/130100595. No secrets between us, right...?``
Shion hesitated for a long time... then, as though resigning herself, she spoke.
``...I'm sorry.@k@vS02/06/130600407. ...I, um... I wasn't trying to hide it.@k@vS02/06/130600408. ...It's just, the conversation sort of got ahead of me, and...``
``...We're in this together, aren't we?!@k@vS02/01/130100597. I won't get angry, so just be honest with me...``
...She still wavered for a few moments.@k ...Then, with an air of resignation, she confessed.
``I... .........told old man Kimiyoshi... ...everything.``
Shion seemed to expect me to yell at her, and I heard her hold her breath.
...But contrary to what she feared... I was actually a little relieved.
...Shion had done the exact same thing I did when I confided in Rika-chan.
...So... choosing my words gently, so as not to upset her... I spoke.
``......Old man Kimiyoshi... he's someone you feel safe confiding in, Shion...?``
``.........Yes.@k@vS02/06/130600411. ...He always... ever since I was little, he was so kind to me...``
......Shion was just as anguished over the mayor's disappearance as the rest of us.
...Though she possessed a strength different from Mion's... her voice now sounded truly sorrowful and pained.
``...I was always playing pranks on him... but he just smiled every time...@k@vS02/06/130600413. ...He'd listen to anything I had to say...@k@vS02/06/130600414. ...He was such a kind person......``
``...C-Calm down, Shion.@k@vS02/01/130100600. ...It's not like he's dead, right?@k@vS02/01/130100601. Don't give up so easily...``
......Shion didn't answer.
...Even as I said those words myself... I didn't believe we'd ever find the missing mayor again.
...Most likely... we wouldn't even learn whether he was alive or dead.
...Shion must have been thinking the same thing...

``......I told the mayor...@k@vS02/06/130600416. ...That night...@k@vS02/06/130600417. that we sneaked into the ritual storehouse.@k@vS02/06/130600418. ...That someone must have seen us... and that there were people after us.``
``...Did the mayor know about the way Takano-san and Tomitake-san died...?``
changeReason: Old uses 'absurdly' not in Japanese; also tense difference (present vs past).

TextENGNew: ``......Yes.@k@vS02/06/130600420. ...He knew.@k@vS02/06/130600421. ...I told him those two died because of Oyashiro-sama's curse... and that I might be the next sacrifice offered to quell his wrath.@k@vS02/06/130600422. ...I told him everything, point-blank.``

``...Old man Kimiyoshi... he wasn't angry.@k@vS02/06/130600424. ......He smiled and said that if Shion-chan was truly sorry, there was no way she'd be spirited away...@k@vS02/06/130600425. ...He really was... smiling... and he said to leave it to... him...... ...ngh...!!``
changeReason: Old uses first-person 'if I was sorry'; new uses third-person 'if Shion-chan was sorry' reflecting mayor's quote. Also 鬼隠し rendered differently.

TextENGNew: Until Shion's sobs subsided... I couldn't find a single word to say.
I couldn't imagine what kind of person the mayor was, or what his relationship with Shion had been like.
...But I knew how much the words 'it'll be all right' meant to someone on the verge of being crushed by fear...@k ...Rika-chan had shown me that very thing today...
changeReason: Old uses generic 'Words of comfort'; new specifies 'the words it'll be all right,' directly referencing the specific phrase 大丈夫 used in context.

TextENGNew: If someone who had given you that kind of strength were to suddenly vanish......
...I could only begin to imagine the shock Shion must have felt...
``............It's my fault.@k@vS02/06/130600427. ...Because I... I told him everything...``
``...Stop that, Shion...@k@vS02/01/130100606. ...It's not your fault.``

``No!!@k@vS02/06/130600429. It IS my fault!!@k@vS02/06/130600430. Because I... I told him...!@k@vS02/06/130600431. Old man Kimiyoshi... he found out...!@k@vS02/06/130600432. That's why... he must have been killed!!``
``It was right after I told him, don't you see?!@k@vS02/06/130600434. The very same night I confessed to him... the night he told me everything would be all right... he vanished!!!``
If someone finds out,@k@|@y......they get dragged in.
changeReason: Old uses past tense about a specific person ('He found out, so he became involved'); new uses conditional/general ('If someone finds out, they get dragged in'), which better matches the JPN generalized curse-like statement.

TextENGNew: ``I never should have told him!!!@k@vS02/06/130600436. This was supposed to be our burden alone! Just the four of us!!@k@vS02/06/130600437. It wasn't something I should have told anyone else!!@k@vS02/06/130600438. He was killed because I talked!@k@vS02/06/130600439. He was killed because he learned the truth!
changeReason: Old translates 咎 as 'fault'; new translates it as 'burden.' JPN 咎 means sin/transgression/blame; 'burden' shifts the meaning from culpability to weight of responsibility.

TextENGNew: ``Because I confessed... he was killed!!!!``

``Wait a minute!!@k@vS02/01/130100608. If they were that thorough... they would obviously have killed you before the mayor!@k@vS02/01/130100609. The order doesn't make sense!!@k@vS02/01/130100610. You or I should have been killed first!!@k@vS02/01/130100611. There's no reason someone else would be killed before us!!!``
``There IS an order!!@k@vS02/06/130600442. They must be planning to save us for last!!``
changeReason: Old says 'No, it's in order' (disagreeing that order is wrong); new says 'There IS an order!' (asserting an order exists). JPN 順番はあります literally means 'there is an order,' making the new version more accurate.

TextENGNew: ``...Huh...? Wh... what did you say?!``

``...They're not just killing us outright... they're killing the people close to us first, one by one... making us suffer as much as possible before they finally come for us... that has to be what they're doing!!``
changeReason: Old says 'not killing the first people who come to mind'; new says 'not just killing us outright.' JPN ひと思いに means 'in one stroke/mercifully quick'; new captures the meaning of 'not giving a quick death' vs old which misconstrues the phrase.

TextENGNew: ``I said calm down!!@k@vS02/01/130100614. You're in shock, Shion! You're panicking!!@k@vS02/01/130100615. The mayor's disappearance has nothing to do with you!@k@vS02/01/130100616. There's no connection!@k@vS02/01/130100617. It has absolutely nothing to do with Takano-san and Tomitake-san's deaths either!!``
...My words came out like a scream... as though I were trying to convince myself.

``No!!@k@vS02/06/130600445. They ARE connected!!@k@vS02/06/130600446. They have to be!!@k@vS02/06/130600447. I told him, and he was killed!@k@vS02/06/130600448. He found out, and he was killed!@k@vS02/06/130600449. I confessed to him...... and he was killed!!!!``
She repeated the same words as before, like a feverish incantation.
changeReason: Old describes Shion's state ('in a delirium'); new describes the quality of her words ('like a feverish incantation'), shifting from speaker's mental state to the character of the speech itself.

TextENGNew: ...Even as I tried to console the frantic Shion... for some reason, the shadow at my feet began to turn to ice......

From within that shadow... a frigid arm reached out--so cold that a mere touch would freeze my very heart--and seized my ankle.@k ......All the warmth in my body... was being drained away through that grip......
It was a bone-chilling... cold.@k ...A freezing, numbing cold.@k ...An absolute... paralyzing cold born of pure terror.
...While I sympathized with Shion, some part of me had been thinking of it as someone else's problem...
changeReason: Old says 'only she had to worry about' (Shion specific); new uses idiom 'someone else's problem' matching JPN 対岸の火事 (literally 'fire on the opposite bank'), a Japanese idiom for distant/others' troubles.

TextENGNew: ...In my head, Shion's eerie words kept repeating, over and over.
He Was Killed Because I Told Him.
He was killed because he found out.
I Confessed Everything To Him...... And He Was Killed.
Those words, chanting like a prayer, like a curse... and when their flat repetition finally took on meaning--
changeReason: Old says 'monotone mantra'; new distinguishes 'prayer' and 'curse' matching JPN 祝詞(norito/prayer) and 呪文(curse/spell), preserving both specific religious/magical references.

TextENGNew: ...The trembling that had refused to stop... surged up my spine as a wave of pure dread!!
``I... I also... the truth is... I... I told her.@k@vS02/01/130100621. ...I told Rika-chan... today!!``
``Rika-chan... you mean Rika-chama?@k@vS02/06/130600453. From the Furude Shrine?``
``Yes, Rika-chan!!@k@vS02/01/130100623. ...I was... so scared, so anxious... I couldn't help it.@k@vS02/01/130100624. ...I told Rika-chan everything.``
``......And... what did Rika-chama say...?``
...The kitty cat is worrying too much.@k I'll definitely do something about it, I promise.
That's what Rika-chan said... and then she smiled.
...She smiled, just the way the missing mayor had smiled to reassure Shion...
``......I-I'm sorry, Shion!@k@vS02/01/130100626. I... I'm suddenly... really worried about Rika-chan...!!``
changeReason: Old says 'a bit worried'; new says 'suddenly really worried,' conveying significantly greater urgency and intensity that better matches the JPN ちょっと (which here is hedging, not minimizing).

TextENGNew: ``Oh, of course!@k@vS02/06/130600456. ...If you're that worried, then please, go ahead.@k@vS02/06/130600457. Call her and make sure she's safe.``
changeReason: Old says 'don't worry about me'; new says 'go ahead.' JPN doesn't include anything about 'don't worry about me' -- that's an addition in the old translation.

TextENGNew: ``...Y-Yeah!! I'll do that!``
``I'll call you again tomorrow around this same time.@k@vS02/06/130600459. Use that as confirmation that I'm still safe.``
``...Understood. I'll be waiting.@k@vS02/01/130100629. Talk to you later!@k@vS02/01/130100630. Sorry!@k@vS02/01/130100631. I'm hanging up!!``
I put down the receiver without waiting for Shion's parting words.
...The danger looming over Shion and me was no longer my primary concern.
......Some primal sixth sense was sounding alarm bells in my head.
...This feeling is bad.@k Really, truly bad.@k ...Without question... bad!!!
``...Damn it... please be safe...@k@vS02/01/130100633. ...Rika-chan!!!``
